items.title: Flash Flood Warning issued March 24 at 12:58AM HST until March 24 at 4:30AM HST by NWS Honolulu HI
items.summary:
FFWHFO

The National Weather Service in Honolulu has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The island of Maui in Maui County

* Until 430 AM HST.

* At 1258 AM HST, radar indicated heavy rain over the eastern slopes
of Haleakala, with rainfall rates of 1 to 3 inches per hour. Flash
flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by heavy rain.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flooding in drainages, streams, rivers, roads,
properties, and other low-lying areas. Public
items.area: Maui, HI

items.title: Fire Weather Watch issued March 24 at 4:40AM MDT until March 26 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Albuquerque NM
items.summary:
...FIRE WEATHER WATCH THURSDAY FROM 11 AM UNTIL 8 PM MDT FOR THE
SANGRE DE CRISTO MOUNTAINS, NORTHEAST HIGHLANDS AND PLAINS, AND
CENTRAL HIGHLANDS DUE TO STRONG WINDS AND LOW HUMIDITY...

.Amidst an extended stretch of record high temperatures, winds
will strengthen on Thursday in response to an upper level trough
passing eastward along the Colorado and New Mexico border, and a
potent surface trough in the lee of the Sangre de Cristo
Mountains. Winds will gust up to 40 mph as humidities plummet
items.area: Northeast Plains

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued March 24 at 2:47AM PDT until March 24 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Medford OR
items.summary:
* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, south winds 25 to 35 kt with gusts
up to 45 kt and seas 7 to 12 ft at 11 seconds expected. For the
Small Craft Advisory, southwest winds 15 to 25 kt and seas 9 to
12 ft at 11 seconds expected.

* WHERE...All of the waters.

* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until 5 PM PDT this afternoon.
For the Small Craft Advisory, from 5 PM this afternoon to 11
PM PDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Strong winds and very steep seas could capsize or
damage vessels. Low visibility conditi
items.area: Coastal waters from Florence to Cape Blanco OR out 10 nm; Waters from Florence to Cape Blanco OR from 10 to 60 nm

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 24 at 5:43AM EDT by NWS Albany NY
items.summary:
Recent snow showers combined with cold temperatures in the 20s have
resulted in areas of black ice in the southern Greens. Motorists
are urged to remain alert for slippery and hazardous travel. Roads
that look wet may in fact be icy. Slow down, leave extra room
between you and cars around you, and plan extra time for your
morning commute.
items.area: Bennington; Western Windham

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 24 at 5:38AM EDT by NWS Burlington VT
items.summary:
Recent snow showers combined with cold temperatures have resulted
in areas of black ice and snow covered roads across portions of
Vermont. Motorists are urged to remain alert for slippery and
hazardous travel. Roads that look wet may in fact be icy. Slow
down, leave extra room between you and cars around you, and plan
extra time for your morning commute.
items.area: Western Franklin; Orleans; Essex; Western Chittenden; Lamoille; Caledonia; Washington; Western Addison; Orange; Western Rutland; Eastern Franklin; Eastern Chittenden; Eastern Addison; Eastern Rutland; Western Windsor; Eastern Windsor

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 24 at 4:12AM CDT by NWS Corpus Christi TX
items.summary:
Areas of fog have developed across portions of South Texas
this morning. Expect visibilities generally 1 mile or less
with a few locations seeing visibilities 1/4 mile or less
at times.

Use caution if traveling as visibility may change rapidly
in a short amount of time. Use low beam headlights and leave
extra distance between your vehicle and others on the road.
items.area: Goliad; Victoria; Jim Wells; Inland Kleberg; Inland Nueces; Inland San Patricio; Coastal Aransas; Inland Refugio; Inland Calhoun; Coastal Kleberg; Coastal Nueces; Coastal San Patricio; Aransas Islands; Coastal Refugio; Coastal Calhoun; Kleberg Islands; Nueces Islands; Calhoun Islands

items.title: High Wind Warning issued March 24 at 3:08AM MDT until March 25 at 6:00PM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...West winds 35 to 45 mph, with gusts up to 65 mph expected.

* WHERE...Eastern Glacier, Western Toole, and Central Pondera
Counties, and Eastern Toole and Liberty Counties.

* WHEN...From 3 AM to 6 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult, especially for high profile
vehicles. Blowing dust could significantly reduce visibility at
times.
items.area: Eastern Glacier, Western Toole, and Central Pondera; Eastern Toole and Liberty

items.title: High Wind Warning issued March 24 at 3:08AM MDT until March 25 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 30 to 40 mph, with gusts up to 60 mph
expected.

* WHERE...The Interstate 90 Corridor between Bozeman and Whitehall,
The Madison and Beaverhead River Valleys, Big Belt, Bridger and
Castle Mountains, Ruby Mountains and Southern Beaverhead
Mountains, and Gallatin and Madison County Mountains and
Centennial Mountains.

* WHEN...From 9 AM to 7 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult, especially for high profile
vehicles.
items.area: Big Belt, Bridger and Castle Mountains; Missouri Headwaters; Madison River Valley; Gallatin Valley; Beaverhead and Western Madison below 6000ft; Ruby Mountains and Southern Beaverhead Mountains; Gallatin and Madison County Mountains and Centennial Mountains

items.title: High Wind Warning issued March 24 at 3:08AM MDT until March 25 at 7:00PM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...West winds 30 to 40 mph, with gusts up to 60 mph expected.

* WHERE...Hill County and Northern Blaine County.

* WHEN...From 9 AM to 7 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult, especially for high profile
vehicles. Blowing dust could significantly reduce visibility at
times.
items.area: Hill County; Northern Blaine County

items.title: Dense Fog Advisory issued March 24 at 4:07AM CDT until March 24 at 11:00AM CDT by NWS Grand Forks ND
items.summary:
* WHAT...Visibility of less then a quarter mile in dense fog.

* WHERE...Lake Of The Woods and Roseau Counties.

* WHEN...Until 11 AM CDT this morning.

* IMPACTS...Low visibility could make driving conditions hazardous.
items.area: Roseau; Lake Of The Woods

items.title: Gale Warning issued March 24 at 1:29AM PDT until March 24 at 11:00PM PDT by NWS Seattle WA
items.summary:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 25 to 35 kt.

* WHERE...Central U. S. Waters Strait Of Juan De Fuca.

* WHEN...From 10 AM this morning to 11 PM PDT this evening.

* IMPACTS...Strong winds will cause hazardous seas which could
capsize or damage vessels and reduce visibility.
items.area: Central U.S. Waters Strait Of Juan De Fuca

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued March 24 at 1:29AM PDT until March 25 at 5:00AM PDT by NWS Seattle WA
items.summary:
* WHAT...Combined seas 7 to 9 feet building to 10 to 12 feet
this afternoon. Bar conditions moderate becoming rough.
Maximum ebb currents will occur around 900 AM Tuesday morning,
945 PM Tuesday evening, and 1015 AM Wednesday morning. The
Tuesday morning ebb will be strong.

* WHERE...Grays Harbor Bar.

* WHEN...From 11 AM this morning to 5 AM PDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft especially
when navigating in or near harbor entrances.
items.area: Grays Harbor Bar

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 24 at 4:21AM EDT by NWS Wilmington NC
items.summary:
The combination of dry fuels, low relative humidity, and gusty
winds will lead to increased fire danger today. Relative humidity
values will drop to 25 to 30 percent, accompanied by northeast winds
of 10 to 15 mph. Gusts to 25 mph are possible this morning.
items.area: Marlboro; Darlington; Dillon; Florence; Marion; Williamsburg; Inland Georgetown; Central Horry; Northern Horry

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued March 24 at 4:05AM EDT until March 25 at 8:00PM EDT by NWS Miami FL
items.summary:
* WHAT...Seas of 6 to 8 feet.

* WHERE...Coastal waters from Jupiter Inlet to Deerfield Beach
FL out 20 NM and Waters from Jupiter Inlet to Deerfield Beach
FL from 20 to 60 NM.

* WHEN...From 4 AM to 8 PM EDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
items.area: Coastal waters from Jupiter Inlet to Deerfield Beach FL out 20 NM; Waters from Jupiter Inlet to Deerfield Beach FL from 20 to 60 NM

items.title: High Wind Watch issued March 24 at 1:36AM MDT until March 25 at 3:00PM MDT by NWS Cheyenne WY
items.summary:
* WHAT...West winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 60 mph possible.

* WHERE...North Snowy Range Foothills including Arlington and Elk
Mountain along Interstate 80.

* WHEN...From 9 AM MDT this morning through Wednesday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...Mainly to transportation. Strong cross winds will be
hazardous to light weight or high profile vehicles, including
campers and tractor trailers.
items.area: North Snowy Range Foothills

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued March 24 at 3:26AM EDT until March 25 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Melbourne FL
items.summary:
* WHAT...Seas 5 to 7 feet expected.

* WHERE...Sebastian Inlet to Jupiter Inlet 0-20 nm.

* WHEN...From 11 PM this evening to 5 PM EDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.
items.area: Sebastian Inlet to Jupiter Inlet 0-20 nm
